title: Formatting Citations
concept: Prompting the generator to cite source page numbers and verifying citations match context.

exercises.prompt: What should you do with the top-3 pieces of information from a PDF before asking for answers?

options.text: Ignore them and ask directly for your question.
Compile them into the context section of the instruction prompt.
Ask your question without any context.

exercises.explain: The correct step is to compile the top-3 pieces of information into the context section, so the bot can use relevant details in its responses.

cards.title: Understanding Citations
cards.body: Citations are important because they tell us where a piece of information comes from. They help verify the accuracy of the information.

cards.title: What is a Citation?
cards.body: A citation is a reference to the source of information, including details like the author, title, and page number.

cards.title: Citations are like Addresses
cards.body: Just like an address leads you to a specific location, citations lead you to the original source of information.

cards.title: Example of a Citation
cards.body: Example: Smith, J. (2021). Learning about AI. New York: Knowledge Press, p. 45.

cards.title: Myth: Citations are Optional
cards.body: Some people think citations are just optional, but they are crucial for academic and professional writing.

cards.title: Citing Sources Correctly
cards.body: Citations must include the author's name, year, and page number.

exercises.prompt: What should a well-formed citation include?

options.text: Author, year, title, and page number
Only the title and page number
The author's name and the date of the last revision

exercises.explain: A well-formed citation must include the author's name, the year of publication, the title, and the page number to reference the source accurately.

exercises.prompt: True or False: It's important to check that citations match the context of the information.
exercises.explain: True! Citations should match the information they reference to ensure accuracy and reliability.

exercises.prompt: Citations help verify the ______ of the information.
exercises.sentence: Citations help verify the accuracy of the information.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'accuracy', as citations confirm that the presented information is correct.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about citations is AI-generated?

options.text: Citations serve as a map leading to the source.
Citations are only for academic writing.

exercises.explain: The correct statement is that citations serve as a map leading to the source, ensuring the information can be traced back.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of including page numbers in citations?

options.text: To help readers find the original source easily.
To make citations look longer and more complicated.
To confuse the readers about who the author is.
To indicate how popular the source is.

exercises.explain: The primary purpose of including page numbers in citations is to help readers find the original source easily. This enhances the credibility of your work and provides a clear path to verify information.
